title: Per-source license + consent audit
context: Redistribution and reprocessing decisions hinge on a rigorous, two-reviewer license + consent audit of every catalogued source (PLAN §7). This gate prevents license/consent violations before any data is reprocessed or redistributed. Binding guardrails: open-access / aggregate / de-identified data only; controlled-access (dbGaP/EGA/biobanks) and identifiable patient data are out of scope; COSMIC/OncoKB/MSigDB-restricted are recipe-reference only; no clinical/diagnostic/prognostic/treatment language; research-only, not medical advice; provenance on every assertion.
objective: Audit every source in the dataset catalog for license, consent terms and redistribution status, recording a per-source ruling with rationale and a second-reviewer verification.
acceptanceCriteria: Every source in the catalog has a license field, a licenseVerifiedBy (second reviewer), and a redistributionAllowed ruling with written rationale.
COSMIC/OncoKB/MSigDB-restricted sources are explicitly marked recipe-reference-only; dbGaP/EGA/biobank sources are marked out-of-scope.
Any ambiguous-consent or ambiguous-license dataset is marked default-exclude pending resolution.
Audit is committed as a reviewable document with per-source provenance.
output: output/license-audit.md (per-source license + consent rulings with second-reviewer verification).
